Not every dream becomes reality.
As many of you know, Iâve been working with a team to find a new location for The Borderaland 2019 (Thatâs NEXT year). Unfortunately, we have just gotten a negative decision from Cementa, the owner of the SmÃ¶jen quarry. They have done an environmental assessment and come to the conclusion that an event like The Borderland is inconsistent with their long term plans for environmental restoration of the quarry. Weâve really tried to find a way around this, but it seems like there isnât much we can do.

This means we need to renew our efforts to search around the Nordics for a place to move The Borderland in 2019. It will be the fourth time we move. Itâs happened before and it will happen again, but it needs all of us to step up and start scouting. Please, take initiative! Help build a future for our community. Iâm checking out for now. It is time for someone else take the torch and march towards that new land on the Border of reality and dreams!

Weâre doing or first NFT drop!
This is Imbued with Love. It is a 110x90cm wall hanging artwork made from optical fiber textile. It is connected to 99 NFT Weaves, digital tokens that allow their owners to go through a ritual and Imbue the artwork with their own interpretation of love. Each new owner gets to charge the art with even more intention and devotion.
These words are stored on the blockchain forever. The artwork itself connects via Wi-Fi and uses these words of Love to generate the patterns of light and color you see in this video.
What you see when you look at this artwork is the loving words of hundreds, eventually thousands of people, expressed through trough technology. The threads of the weave, becoming tokens of love.
On Friday at 18.00 CET , we release the first 40 NFTs at www.imbuedart.com. Imbued with Love will be auctioned out starting Dec 27.

Kram pÃ¥ er â¤ï¸

This is Imbued with Love. No filters. No photoshop. Weâre just fading the light in the room.
It is 110x90cm, created from optical fibre textiles, woven specifically for this piece. The pattern in the fabric comes from a knitted dress made by @malinbobecktadaaâs mother mother for our daughter, each stitch an expression of generational love. 
The patterns of light were made by me, and it uses words of love stored on the blockchain to generate its colors. As the digital tokens connected to Love change hands, each new caretaker will get to express their interpretations of Love and change the appearance of the artwork.
When you look at Imbued with Love, what you see is the expressions of Love from hundreds, or thousands of people. Some strangers, some family. All there, in the moment.
Weâre almost ready for the exhibition, which will open on December 10 at Hotel El Ganzo, San Jose del Cabo, Mexico. It will then be put on auction on December 27, live streamed through our webpage.
#art #fineart #nftart #trippyart #ledart #artwork #artofinstagram #artistsoninstagram #imbuedwithlove

Imbued with Love is a physical artwork that connects in real-time, via Wi-Fi, to the blockchain. It is made from custom woven optical fibre textiles, illuminated by LEDs. It connects to Non-Fungible Tokens, whose owners are invited to go through a ritual where they put their interpretation of Love into words. These are stored on the Ethereum blockchain and are used to create the color and light that flows through the artwork itself.

*âI want those who view my art, to become a part of it. Every viewer will have their own interpretation. But what if these interpretations could be permanently woven into the artwork itself?â  - Malin Bobeck Tadaa*

Imbued with Love is the first in a series of seven artworks. Each is imbued by the artist with an emotion, intertwining her own memories and rituals into the pieces. Each is accompanied by 99 NFT Weaves, created from pieces of the original weave files. The NFTs invite people across the world to Imbue their own interpretations of these emotions into the art.

The project uses the technology and philosophical framework of blockchain technology to shed light on the history of textile. It takes us back to a time when fabric existed as something personal, and was imbued with memories.

*"When you look at Imbued with Love, what you see is the loving intentions of a hundred people spread across the world." - Malin Bobeck Tadaa*

Imbued with Love will be exhibited at Hotel El Ganzo, San Jose del Cabo, Mexico. It will be auctioned out on www.imbuedart.com on Dec 27 - Jan 2.

I'm looking for mentorship from someone who truly understands the use of nested sine waves in graphics coding.
I've been coding led effects for a decade. I write everything from scratch in FastLED, and my main tool have always been nesting and factoring cosine waves. I write a nice formula, stick time and x-value in there and see what happens. I tinker with different factors, until it looks like how I want it.
Now I'm doing an artwork where the last pixel connect to the first, so all effects I do have to wrap around nicely. I can make it happen, but it's at that point I realise I don't actually know what I'm doing. What do all the cosines in cos(cos()*cos()+cos()) actually mean? :D
Is there anyone who knows this area of math in depth, and who would be willing to do a call and help me sort out my brain?
Thank you!
/ Gustaf

Once again inviting all you men out there to ManKind Projects New Warrior Training Adventure. Itâs a 48 hour training that have given me so much, both as a participant and as staff.
Itâs extremely well held, with 60 staff men for 35 participants, including staff who have done the same training 50 times. Itâs non-profit, with staff men even covering their own costs to be able to contribute.

This is an invitation to ask yourself what kind of man you want to be: To build integrity, hold yourself accountable and take responsibility for your own bullshit. It is not easy, but it is one of the best things I've done. It has made me a better father, husband, friend and co-worker - a better man.

The next training is September 15-17.

Please message me if you want to know more, or sign up at http://www.mkpnordic.org/nwta

- - - - Andetag no. 4 - - - - 
andetag [ËanËdetaËÉ¡]
From ande (en. spirit) + tag (en. take).
Swedish noun.
breath, an inhalation and exhalation of air from the lungs.
Andetag is a visual manifestation of a deep breath, inviting people around the world to breathe in unison. The artworks are sculpted from custom woven textiles, animated with light to illustrate the flow of air through the lungs.
The animation allows the viewer to synchronize their breath with the artwork. Every piece is synchronized via the internet to every other, a global rhythm, gathering the world in one collective breath.
#fineart #art #andetag #breath #breathwork #opticalfibre #ledart #textileart

Video by @[512384175:2048:Johan Jb Eriksson] / TERRAN

**TL;DR:** Iâd like to share some love for David Roos and The Mothership. David is someone who cares deeply about our community and is doing his best to navigate our principles and ways of working. Heâs generous, curious, and ambitious. He genuinely wants to do good. He deserves to be listened to.

---

David and I had only met very briefly before The Borderland last yearâwe really didnât know each other at all.

Like many others, I was at times annoyed by the sound from The Mothership during the event. I was also triggered by their use of Borderland photography on their Instagram account. But unlike everyone else, I decided to reach out to David and ask if he wanted to talk about it.

I quickly realized I had gotten so many things wrong. Our conversation shed light on some deeper issues within our community. And I got to know someone who is truly, deeply tryingâputting in his time, energy, and money to contribute.

I also got to take a look at the unkind side of The Borderland.

It saddens me how quick we are, as a community, to assume the worst in each other. Itâs okay to disagree. Itâs okay to call each other out when mistakes are made. But if we want to thrive and grow, we have to trust thatâeven when we donât know each other personallyâmost of us have good intentions. We need to be curious before we judge. Admittedly, it is something I struggle with myself.

After last yearâs event, many people publicly criticised David and The Mothership. But no one reached out to actually talk to him, to understand his perspective.

So I met with him again, this time to film our conversation. The video isnât polishedâitâs long, unedited, and a bit rambly. But itâs real. Itâs us. And we wanted to share it with you.

If you donât watch the video, thatâs fine. All I ask is: stay curious. Give people a chance. Assume good intentions.

And if you still disagree with someoneâs actions, ask yourself: 

*âHow might I support them?â*

Thatâs how we build community.

videos_v2.description:
It took about 10 000 LED pixels, 200 meters of custom woven optical fibre textile (that's 112 km/70 miles of optical fibre), to create a 225 sqm breathing, immersive art museum.

I wanted to share this little Behind The Scenes video with you ð

Thank you for all the inspiration and advice I've gotten from this group. If anyone in this group is ever in Stockholm, feel free to hit me up and I'll show you around @[61574989074329:2048:Andetag]

I often struggle to connect with digital art shown on screens. It feels like Iâm not really looking at the art, Iâm looking at a screen displaying art.

Iâm searching for ways to express the dynamic, generative nature of digital art, while still staying solidly grounded in physical reality.â - @gustaftadaa

We live in a world full of technology â
but starved for connection.
At ANDETAG, something shifts.
You slow down. You feel present. You feelâ¦ connected.

Step in. Lie down.
